title: Add distributed view counter with eventual consistency
description: Implements a distributed view counter using Redis INCR across multiple app instances with periodic flush to PostgreSQL.
diff:
diff --git a/services/view_counter.py b/services/view_counter.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..f6a7b8c
--- /dev/null
+++ b/services/view_counter.py
@@ -0,0 +1,28 @@
+from __future__ import annotations
+from redis import Redis
+from database import get_session
+
+class ViewCounter:
+    def __init__(self, redis: Redis) -> None:
+        self._redis = redis
+
+    def increment(self, page_id: str) -> int:
+        return self._redis.incr(f"views:{page_id}")
+
+    def flush_to_db(self) -> None:
+        session = get_session()
+        keys = self._redis.keys("views:*")
+        for key in keys:
+            page_id = key.decode().split(":", 1)[1]
+            count = int(self._redis.get(key) or 0)
+            # Non-atomic: another instance could INCR between GET and DELETE
+            self._redis.delete(key)
+            session.execute(
+                "UPDATE pages SET view_count = view_count + %s WHERE id = %s",
+                (count, page_id)
+            )
+        session.commit()
comments: fay: Nice distributed counter pattern. Approve.

greg: The flush_to_db has a race condition. Between redis.get(key) and redis.delete(key), other instances can INCR the same key. Those increments are lost when delete runs. Use GETDEL or a Lua script for atomic get-and-reset.
helen: Also, redis.keys('views:*') is O(N) and blocks the Redis event loop. Use SCAN instead.
author-ivan: I'll use GETDEL and switch to SCAN.
author: ivan
